title: euphrasia officinalis subsp. pratensis
intro: Euphrasia officinalis subsp. pratensis, also known as the common or marsh eyebright, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its small, blue or purple flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating eye infections and other conditions. Euphrasia officinalis subsp. pratensis is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting healthy digestion, as well as providing antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to soothe eye irritation and promote overall well-being.
